2025-01-24.thesis: NASA awards Intuitive Machines $4.82B lunar comms contract Jan 24
2025-01-24.exit_case: The $4.82B NASA contract was formally awarded Jan 24 — the specific named catalyst has occurred, is public, and LUNR is already skyrocketing on it.
2025-01-24.assessment: This is an acute, datable catalyst: a specific named government contract award ($4.82B NASA lunar comms). It was awarded Jan 24 and is now fully public — Motley Fool has it as a front-page 'why is this stock skyrocketing today' piece, confirming the news is widely distributed. Per the rules, a named deal that has closed/been awarded is catalyst_resolved=true, and thesis_live must therefore be false. The stock's intraday surge shows the market has received and is pricing the information in real time. No informational edge remains on this specific event. If a fresh concrete milestone emerges (e.g., a specific large task order awarded, a mission launch contract signed under this umbrella), that would warrant a new event entry.
2025-01-24.exit_advice: EXIT. The precise catalyst entered on — a named NASA contract award — has resolved on entry day. LUNR is already sharply reacting to the public announcement. There is no pre-announcement edge remaining. Any future thesis (task orders, mission milestones, contract modifications) would be a new, separate catalyst, not this one.

2025-02-14.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-02-14.exit_case: The 'opening tariff' shock has already landed; the initial safe-haven gold spike may already be priced in.
2025-02-14.assessment: Week 1. Trump's tariffs on China, Canada, and Mexico are live and unresolved, layering a trade-war safe-haven bid on top of record central-bank gold buying and a wobbling dollar — a multi-leg structural driver, not a one-off event. Investing.com explicitly tags GDX as 'still-under-owned,' confirming early-stage positioning. The arc is not complete: further tariff escalation (sector-specific, retaliatory rounds) is a concrete open possibility, and the dollar/risk-off dynamic is ongoing. All four vehicles (GDX as leveraged miner ETF, GOLD/Barrick, AEM/Agnico, NEM/Newmont) cleanly express the catalyst. The fool.ca piece on Canadian energy is off-thesis; disregard. Conviction 7: specific named catalyst, clear pure-plays, press explicitly flagging the under-owned angle — docked from 8 because gold safe-haven is a well-worn narrative that risks fast crowding.
2025-02-14.exit_advice: Hold. The tariffs are still in force — not reversed, not lifted — making this a structural/ongoing driver, not a single datable event that passed. Exit only on a concrete reversal: tariffs rescinded, a negotiated framework signed, or a sustained dollar recovery that kills the safe-haven bid. Do not exit on silence or a quiet week.

2025-02-21.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-02-21.exit_case: Gold nearing record highs signals safe-haven surge is mainstream-priced, not early-stage anymore.
2025-02-21.assessment: Week 2: Trump's tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico remain live and unresolved — no deal signed, no pause, no retraction. Gold is pressing toward record highs (DailyForex, 2/19), validating the safe-haven bid. However, this week's press is thin and off-vehicle: the Ghana Stock Exchange piece is entirely off-thesis, and the DailyForex note is generic technical analysis with no explicit tariff-miner linkage to GDX, AEM, GOLD, or NEM. Applying -1 silence decay (7→6) for absence of fresh catalyst-specific miner coverage. The structural multi-leg driver — tariffs + central bank buying + dollar wobble — remains intact with no reversal signal. All four vehicles continue to cleanly express the thesis. Devil's advocate case (mainstream pricing) is noted but does not clear the exit bar: tariffs are unresolved and no concrete next-step ceasefire or trade deal is announced, so the arc is not complete and the driver has not reversed.
2025-02-21.exit_advice: Hold. Exit only on a concrete tariff pause/rescission on China, Canada, or Mexico that removes the safe-haven rationale. Do not exit on gold price strength alone.

2025-02-28.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-02-28.exit_case: Gold posts its first weekly loss of 2025 as tariff-driven dollar strength now suppresses, not amplifies, the safe-haven bid.
2025-02-28.assessment: Week 3. Tariffs are still unresolved and explicitly driving market behavior — news.az directly links Trump's tariff plans to gold's first weekly loss of 2025, while investorideas confirms gold is retreating as the dollar strengthens. This is the key new dynamic: tariff uncertainty is now a two-sided force, boosting the dollar (which weighs on gold) while simultaneously sustaining risk-off sentiment. That internal tension does not kill the thesis — it is a temporary mechanical headwind within a live structural driver. Crucially, this week delivers FRESH, explicitly tariff-linked gold coverage (news.az, investorideas), which resets last week's –1 silence-decay penalty; conviction recovers from 6 back toward 7, but the gold pullback and the dollar-headwind complexity temper that recovery, so I hold at 6. The InsiderMonkey 'Best Gold Dividend Stocks' piece adds mild analyst tailwind for AEM/NEM/GOLD. Devil's advocate is noted seriously: if the dollar-strength dynamic becomes the dominant tariff transmission mechanism, gold miners underperform; but this does not clear the exit bar (no deal signed, no tariff lifted). All four vehicles remain clean pure-plays. Structural driver intact — hold.
2025-02-28.exit_advice: Do NOT exit. The pullback is a mechanics shift (dollar strength from tariffs offsetting safe-haven demand), not a catalyst reversal. Tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico remain fully live and unresolved; no deal, pause, or retraction has been announced. The driver has not reversed — hold.

2025-03-07.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-03-07.exit_case: Tariffs went live March 3 — the pending-threat uncertainty premium may now be fully priced and absorbed.
2025-03-07.assessment: Week 4. The Forbes piece (3/3) confirms Trump's tariffs formally went into effect, keeping the structural driver alive — no deal signed, no pause, no reversal. Devil's advocate is taken seriously this week: one could argue the 'pending threat' phase has resolved into implementation, collapsing the uncertainty premium. Counter: for a structural, multi-leg driver, tariffs going live is the opening of the trade-war arc (retaliation rounds, escalation, sector-specific follow-ons still ahead), not its resolution. Catalyst_resolved stays false. More damaging for conviction: this week's news is poor on vehicle-specific signal. Forbes is crypto-focused (not gold miners), Star Phoenix is Canadian political commentary (off-vehicle), and the Brazil article is entirely off-thesis. Zero fresh tariff-gold-miner linkage this week → silence decay applies: conviction 6→5. The structural driver — tariff risk-off + central bank buying + dollar wobble — remains mechanically intact, and all four vehicles continue to cleanly express it. Hold, but the position must compete against stronger-signal events on the shortlist at this lower conviction score.
2025-03-07.exit_advice: Hold; exit only on a concrete US-China/Canada/Mexico trade deal, formal tariff pause, or a sustained dollar-strength-driven gold price breakdown. Do not exit on silence or crowd noise alone.

2025-03-14.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-03-14.exit_case: Tariffs fully implemented; initial uncertainty-driven safe-haven premium may already be absorbed into gold prices.
2025-03-14.assessment: Week 5. The structural driver remains mechanically intact: Trump tariffs are confirmed live and still denting broad market sentiment (NBC LA, NBC Chicago, 3/13), with no deal signed, no pause, no reversal. The devil's advocate case — that the initial uncertainty premium has been fully absorbed now that tariffs are implemented policy rather than a pending threat — is the strongest it has been, but it does not clear the hard exit bar (no reversal of the driving condition). What IS clear: this week's news provides zero explicit tariff-gold-miner linkage. The NBC pieces are generic macro sentiment; Billboard is entirely off-vehicle. Three of the last four weeks have had thin-to-zero vehicle-specific signal, which is compounding silence decay. Applying -1 silence decay: conviction 5→4. The structural driver (tariff risk-off + central bank buying + dollar instability) continues to express itself through all four vehicles, but the early/under-the-radar edge is eroding as this becomes mainstream market noise. All four vehicles (GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM) remain valid pure-plays. Hold at 4 — the event must now out-compete fresher, higher-conviction catalysts on the shortlist.
2025-03-14.exit_advice: Hold but this event is near the cull floor. Exit ONLY if tariffs are lifted, a deal is signed, or gold miners get fresh explicit coverage that resets the signal. Do NOT exit on silence alone, but at conviction 4 this position should lose the fitness contest to higher-signal events.

2025-03-28.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-03-28.exit_case: Gold is at all-time record highs with mainstream coverage; the early 'under-owned' edge from Week 1 is fully consumed.
2025-03-28.assessment: Week 7. Two pieces of fresh relevant coverage partially RESET the compounding silence decay this week: (1) fool.com.au confirms gold shares are rallying on a NEW all-time record for the gold price — directly bullish for every vehicle in the basket — and (2) fxstreet.com explicitly links Trump tariff policies to escalating market uncertainty and risk-off sentiment. The structural driver is not merely intact; it is actively expressing itself through a gold price at record highs. Silence decay reverses: conviction steps from 3 back to 5. The reset is not to entry-level conviction (7) because: the early/under-the-radar angle is clearly consumed (gold at records = mainstream consensus, no longer under-owned), and the week's coverage, while fresh, is not directly vehicle-specific (ASX miners rather than GDX/AEM/GOLD/NEM). The devil's advocate case this week is the strongest it has been — gold at records with universal press coverage is the definition of a priced-in trade — but it does NOT clear the hard exit bar: tariffs are live, no deal or ceasefire is signed, no pause is announced, and fxstreet frames further tariff escalation as a concrete forward risk-off driver. Mild tariff optimism noted (marketscreener) but it is vague and general, not a concrete reversal event. Forbes (banker bonuses), Investopedia (S&P cuts from Barclays — tariff-driven but not gold-specific), Indian market wraps, and Goldman tech calls are all off-vehicle. All four vehicles — GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM — remain valid pure-plays on rising gold prices and safe-haven demand; no individual vehicle thesis has collapsed. Hold at conviction 5; basket unchanged.
2025-03-28.exit_advice: Do not exit: tariffs remain live and unresolved, no deal or pause signed, and fxstreet explicitly links Trump tariff policy to ongoing risk-off escalation. The priced-in case is real but does not clear the hard-exit bar — the driving condition has not reversed.

2025-04-04.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-04-04.exit_case: Liberation Day broad equity rout forces gold-miner liquidation alongside risk assets; safe-haven premium temporarily overwhelmed by margin calls.
2025-04-04.assessment: Week 8. Trump's 'Liberation Day' (April 2) tariffs — the broadest U.S. tariff action in roughly a century — represent a massive escalation of the original structural driver, not its resolution. Multiple outlets confirm a global equity rout ($100B+ wiped from Australian markets alone; railroad stocks collapsing), validating that tariff-driven risk-off sentiment is now at peak intensity. The devil's advocate case is real but short-term: in severe broad-market selloffs, gold miners are sometimes sold alongside equities in the first wave as investors raise cash, temporarily breaking the safe-haven premium. This is a mechanical headwind, not a thesis reversal — gold itself should benefit as the shock propagates. The arc is plainly not complete: Chinese/EU retaliation, sector-specific follow-ons, and negotiation failures all remain concrete forward catalysts. Silence decay does NOT apply this week: Liberation Day is a fresh, concrete, datable escalation milestone in the structural driver. Conviction steps from 5 to 6 — partial recovery reflecting the magnitude of the catalyst acceleration, held short of a full reset because vehicle-specific gold/miner press is absent from this week's feed. The early/under-the-radar edge is consumed but the STRUCTURAL driver is actively intensifying, keeping thesis_live. All four vehicles (GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM) remain valid pure-plays on safe-haven gold demand.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Hold. Liberation Day tariffs are the largest single concrete escalation of the structural driver since entry — the opposite of resolution. No deal signed, no pause, no tariff lifted. Retaliation rounds from China/EU and sector-specific follow-ons are concrete next steps still ahead.

2025-04-11.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-04-11.exit_case: Trump paused most country-specific tariffs to 10% baseline for 90 days, partially reversing the multi-country shock catalyst.
2025-04-11.assessment: Week 9. This is the strongest catalyst-signal week since entry. Elkodaily.com explicitly links gold's record $3,200/oz print to tariff-driven 'extreme uncertainty,' with analysts projecting $3,600 — a direct, named catalyst-vehicle linkage that fully resets silence decay and justifies stepping conviction from 6 to 7. The week's macro arc is complex but net-bullish for the thesis: (1) Trump announced a 90-day pause dropping most country-specific tariffs to a universal 10% baseline (Canada, Mexico, EU, India partial suspension) — a partial reversal of the original multi-country catalyst — but (2) US-China tariffs simultaneously ESCALATED to their highest level yet, with Australian equity markets drooping on the China-specific rate revelation and multiple outlets confirming the tariff war is intensifying on its most consequential bilateral leg. The net result: the structural driver has narrowed and intensified. The Canada/Mexico leg is temporarily muted, but the China leg is now the dominant global risk-off force, and gold's response (new record high, $3,200+) unambiguously validates that safe-haven demand is accelerating, not retreating. The 90-day pause is the strongest devil's advocate case of the nine-week run, but it does not clear the hard exit bar: it is explicitly time-limited (90 days, expiring July 9–ish), does not include China, and leaves intact the structural uncertainty premium that is driving gold. The 'extreme uncertainty' framing in fresh press is precisely the driver this thesis entered on. All four vehicles — GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM — remain valid, direct pure-plays on gold miners benefiting from record spot prices. Conviction 7 (fresh vehicle-specific coverage resets decay; held from 8 because the early/under-the-radar edge is consumed and the 90-day pause introduces a genuine partial-reversal risk on the non-China legs).
2025-04-11.exit_advice: Hold — the 90-day pause is temporary, conditional, and explicitly excludes China, where tariffs are escalating; gold hit a new $3,200 record confirming the safe-haven bid is intensifying, not retreating. Hard exit only on a concrete US-China trade deal signed or tariffs formally and durably lifted.

Elkodaily.com explicitly links gold's record $3,200/oz print to tariff-driven 'extreme uncertainty,' with analysts projecting $3,600 — a direct, named catalyst-vehicle linkage that fully resets silence decay and justifies stepping conviction from 6 to 7. The week's macro arc is complex but net-bullish for the thesis: (1) Trump announced a 90-day pause dropping most country-specific tariffs to a universal 10% baseline (Canada, Mexico, EU, India partial suspension) — a partial reversal of the original multi-country catalyst — but (2) US-China tariffs simultaneously ESCALATED to their highest level yet, with Australian equity markets drooping on the China-specific rate revelation and multiple outlets confirming the tariff war is intensifying on its most consequential bilateral leg. The net result: the structural driver has narrowed and intensified. The Canada/Mexico leg is temporarily muted, but the China leg is now the dominant global risk-off force, and gold's response (new record high, $3,200+) unambiguously validates that safe-haven demand is accelerating, not retreating. The 90-day pause is the strongest devil's advocate case of the nine-week run, but it does not clear the hard exit bar: it is explicitly time-limited (90 days, expiring July 9–ish), does not include China, and leaves intact the structural uncertainty premium that is driving gold. The 'extreme uncertainty' framing in fresh press is precisely the driver this thesis entered on. All four vehicles — GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM — remain valid, direct pure-plays on gold miners benefiting from record spot prices. Conviction 7 (fresh vehicle-specific coverage resets decay; held from 8 because the early/under-the-radar edge is consumed and the 90-day pause introduces a genuine partial-reversal risk on the non-China legs).
2025-04-11.exit_advice: Hold — the 90-day pause is temporary, conditional, and explicitly excludes China, where tariffs are escalating; gold hit a new $3,200 record confirming the safe-haven bid is intensifying, not retreating. Hard exit only on a concrete US-China trade deal signed or tariffs formally and durably lifted.

2025-04-18.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-04-18.exit_case: Sequential tariff easings — electronics exempt, auto breaks hinted, Japan talks advancing — suggest peak tariff shock is passing.
2025-04-18.assessment: Week 10. No direct gold/miner coverage this week → silence decay applies: conviction 7→6. The tariff landscape is actively bifurcating. On the easing side: Trump exempted electronics 'for now,' hinted at auto breaks, and US-Japan talks are advancing (multiple outlets, 4/15-17) — partial softening of the original multi-country shock. On the escalation side: EU talks broke down entirely, with Yahoo (4/15) explicitly reporting the EU now faces HIGHER tariffs; US-China remains at its highest-ever level; and the electronics exemption itself carries 'for now' language that preserves optionality for re-escalation. No formal, comprehensive trade deal has been signed; no broad tariff reversal has been announced. The 90-day pause expiry (~July 9) and the unresolved EU/China bilateral arcs remain concrete forward milestones. The devil's advocate case is the strongest it has been in recent weeks: sequential sector- and country-level exemptions are the textbook pattern of a trade war being gradually walked back rather than hardened. However, gradual rollback is not the same as reversal — the structural uncertainty premium (the exact thing gold is pricing) is sustained precisely because the policy is erratic and 'for now.' Gold at $3,200+ (last week's record) with no explicit pullback signal this week confirms the safe-haven bid has not evaporated. The CNBC defensive-positioning piece (4/18) confirms broader market stress from trade policy persists. No individual vehicle thesis has collapsed. Hold at conviction 6; all four vehicles remain valid pure-plays on safe-haven gold demand. A fresh explicit gold/miner-tariff story would reset decay; a concrete broad tariff deal or gold price breakdown would trigger reassessment.
2025-04-18.exit_advice: Hold. No formal deal signed; EU talks broke down and EU now faces higher tariffs; US-China tariffs remain at record levels; 90-day pause expires ~July 9. Structural driver intact despite piecemeal easings on specific sectors/countries.

2025-04-18.thesis: Selected to bid on billions in US/UK government launch contracts
2025-04-18.exit_case: The 'selected to bid' announcement has already occurred and is fully public; however, actual contract awards remain pending and unresolved.
2025-04-18.assessment: First week on the position. The specific catalyst — selection to bid on billions in US and UK government launch contracts — was confirmed for RKLB via Yahoo Finance (2025-04-15), which is what drove the entry. The selection-to-bid is the trigger, not the resolution: the actual contract awards (the value-generating milestones) are still ahead and wholly unresolved, keeping the thesis live. This is a concrete, datable competitive process with a meaningful bilateral government backstop (US + UK), not vague speculation. Coverage is early-stage — one Yahoo Finance story, no mainstream saturation. RKLB is the clean primary pure-play; its selection is explicitly named. LUNR is included as a known vehicle but this week's LUNR coverage is only a generic 'best space stock' roundup with no direct link to the same bidding selection — keep it in the basket but treat it as a weaker secondary until a specific selection announcement surfaces for LUNR on the same contracts. Hold through the competitive process; watch for contract award announcements as the key resolution event.
2025-04-18.exit_advice: Exit if: (a) actual contracts are awarded to competitors rather than RKLB/LUNR, (b) the bidding process is cancelled or materially restructured, or (c) a contract award to RKLB/LUNR is announced and fully priced in with no further award tranches pending.

2025-04-25.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-04-25.exit_case: Trump sequentially easing tariffs (electronics, autos, Canada partial) while equities and Bitcoin surge risk-on, suggesting safe-haven urgency is structurally fading.
2025-04-25.assessment: Week 11. The structural driver remains intact but the macro crosscurrents are the most mixed of the entire run. On the bull side: livemint.com (4/22) explicitly reports gold is in a 'historic run fueled by uncertainty and central bank buying' — this is fresh, vehicle-adjacent catalyst coverage that halts this week's silence decay and holds conviction at 6 rather than stepping it to 5. On the headwind side: Trump is continuing sequential tariff easing (auto break hints, naharnet 4/22); global equities are rallying; and Bitcoin has surged to $94K — collectively the most coherent risk-on shift since the Liberation Day peak, which is precisely the condition that would drain gold's safe-haven premium. This week's devil's advocate case is serious: sequential country- and sector-level exemptions are the textbook pattern of a trade war being gradually unwound. However, the hard exit bar is not cleared — no comprehensive deal has been signed, US-China tariffs remain at their highest-ever level, the 90-day pause expires ~July 9 with no concrete replacement deal in place, and gold's 'beating everything' performance confirms the uncertainty premium is still actively being priced in real time. The structural arc is not complete. All four vehicles — GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM — remain valid pure-plays on safe-haven gold demand. Conviction holds at 6; a formal US-China framework or a sustained gold pullback below $3,100 would force reassessment.
2025-04-25.exit_advice: Hold. The livemint 'Gold is beating everything' piece is fresh, on-thesis coverage that prevents this week's silence decay step-down. Hard exit triggers: a formal US-China deal signed; gold price breaking down materially from record highs; or the 90-day pause converted into a permanent, broad tariff reduction. The sequential-easing pattern is the clearest warning signal to date — watch closely.

The competitive bid process is an acute, datable, concrete milestone still ahead — hold until awards resolve. 2025-05-02.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-05-02.exit_case: US-China trade talk optimism signals potential resolution of the last major live tariff leg.
2025-05-02.assessment: Week 12. The most striking data point this week is the one most bullish for the thesis: Q1 2025 US GDP contracted -0.3% (from +2.4%), the first contraction in three years, explicitly blamed on the tariff-driven import surge (thisdaylive, 5/1). This is among the strongest real-economy validations of the original thesis — tariffs are causing material economic damage that sustains risk-off sentiment and gold's safe-haven premium. The primary headwind is the rttnews piece (5/2): equities surged on jobs data and 'optimism about trade talks with China.' If the China leg — now the dominant engine of the structural driver — shows genuine progress toward resolution, the thesis weakens materially. However, 'optimism' is precisely the speculative framing the exit rules flag as insufficient: no talks are formally scheduled, no framework is announced, and US-China tariffs remain at their highest-ever level. The 90-day pause (~July 9 expiry) still has no replacement deal. The GDP contraction data also cuts against a clean risk-on pivot — a tariff-induced recession sustains gold demand even as equities rally on short-term sentiment. Fresh tariff coverage this week (thisdaylive GDP story) prevents silence decay step-down; conviction holds at 6. The devil's advocate is real — sequential easing signals across multiple weeks are consistent with a trade war gradually unwinding — but the structural driver has not reversed. All four vehicles remain valid pure-plays.
2025-05-02.exit_advice: Hold. 'Optimism about trade talks' is speculative language — no summit scheduled, no framework signed, no tariff lifted. US-China rates remain at record highs; 90-day pause expires ~July 9 with no replacement deal. Exit only on a concrete signed US-China framework or a sustained gold breakdown below $3,100 — not on sentiment headlines.

2025-05-09.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-05-09.exit_case: US-UK formal trade framework deal — first signed bilateral pact of the tariff era — signals Trump willing to resolve disputes, eroding 'intractable trade war' safe-haven premium.
2025-05-09.assessment: Week 13. The dominant story this week is the US-UK trade framework deal, hailed by multiple outlets (Kiplinger, Townhall, The Star, Invezz) as the first formal bilateral deal since Trump's tariff push began, triggering a broad equity rally on 'trade optimism.' This is the single most meaningful headwind to the thesis since the 90-day pause in Week 9, because it validates the 'Trump makes deals' narrative that, if extended to China, would drain gold's safe-haven premium. Devil's advocate is at its strongest in 13 weeks: sequential easing (electronics exemption, auto break hints, Japan talks advancing, now UK deal) fits the textbook pattern of a trade war being gradually unwound rather than hardened. However, the hard exit bar is not cleared: (1) the UK is not among the original catalyst countries (China/Canada/Mexico); (2) the deal is described as a 'framework outline' with lower auto tariffs — not a comprehensive reversal; (3) US-China tariffs remain at their highest-ever level with no formal talks scheduled; (4) the 90-day pause expires ~July 9 with no concrete replacement deal. The structural driver — tariff-induced economic uncertainty sustaining gold's safe-haven premium — has not reversed. Critically, this week's news contains zero explicit gold/miner/tariff-gold coverage: all eight articles are equity market or trade-deal commentary with no linkage to GDX, GOLD, AEM, or NEM. Silence decay applies: conviction 6→5. The cumulative easing signals are building a case for priced-in soft fade, but the China leg — now the dominant engine — keeps the structural driver alive. All four vehicles remain valid pure-plays.
2025-05-09.exit_advice: Hold but watch closely. The US-UK deal is with a non-catalyst country (UK ≠ China/Canada/Mexico) and described as a 'framework outline,' not a fully implemented agreement eliminating the original tariffs. US-China remains at record-high tariff levels; the 90-day pause (~July 9 expiry) on Canada/Mexico has no replacement deal. The structural driver has not reversed. However, if a formal US-China framework is announced — even an outline — reassess immediately: that IS the original catalyst country and would likely mark the end of the arc.

2025-05-16.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-05-16.exit_case: US-China tariff cut from 145% to 30% (announced May 12) materially reverses the dominant driver engine my own journal repeatedly labeled 'the dominant engine.'
2025-05-16.assessment: Week 14. The dominant story — referenced in article.wn.com (May 15) as a past fact ('after the two countries lowered tariffs for each other') — is the US-China tariff truce announced ~May 12: US rates cut from ~145% to ~30%, China from ~125% to ~10%, on a 90-day basis. This is the single most material de-escalation in the entire 14-week run, and it strikes the leg my journal has explicitly called 'the dominant engine' since Week 9. The exit case is the strongest of the run. However, the hard-exit bar is not quite cleared for three reasons: (1) it is a 90-day pause, not a permanent deal — the uncertainty premium survives the expiry horizon; (2) 30% tariffs on Chinese goods are historically very high and still generate pass-through inflation (Walmart price-hike stories confirm this in the current feed); (3) strategic decoupling / 'poison pill' supply-chain provisions (article.wn.com) add a new form of geopolitical pressure that sustains residual safe-haven demand. The Ontario $14.6B deficit piece and Walmart warnings confirm the structural economic damage from tariffs is still propagating, which sustains some gold-demand floor. SILENCE DECAY applies in full: zero explicit gold/miner/tariff-gold linkage in this week's feed — every article is retail, geopolitical, or off-vehicle. Conviction: 5 (prior) → 4 (silence decay) → 3 (China de-escalation materially weakening dominant engine). All four vehicles remain technically valid pure-plays on gold, but the basket is at the soft-exit floor.
2025-05-16.exit_advice: Soft-fade at cull floor. The US-China tariff de-escalation (145%→30%, May 12) is the single most damaging event to this thesis in 14 weeks — the China leg I explicitly called the dominant engine has been throttled ~79%. Silence decay also applies this week (zero gold/miner-specific coverage). The structural driver is not fully reversed — 30% tariffs remain historically high, the pause is 90 days with no permanent deal, strategic decoupling provisions persist, and Q1 GDP damage is already in the pipeline — but the thesis should be out-competed by any fresher catalyst on the shortlist. Do not add to position; allow mechanical culling.

2025-05-23.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-05-23.exit_case: US-China truce slashed the dominant engine 145%→30%; Canada/Mexico leg softened since Week 9; all three original catalyst legs materially de-escalated.
2025-05-23.assessment: Week 15. Zero fresh coverage → silence decay: 3→2. The structural driver is technically alive but has been hollowed out across every leg: the Canada/Mexico leg was softened by the 90-day pause (Week 9); the China leg — explicitly called 'the dominant engine' since Week 9 — was cut from ~145% to ~30% in the May 12 truce (Week 14). The original three-pronged catalyst (China/Canada/Mexico igniting a safe-haven surge) has been substantially de-escalated without a permanent reversal. What keeps catalyst_resolved=false and thesis_live=true: (1) 30% tariffs on China remain historically elevated and pass-through inflation persists; (2) the 90-day arrangements create two concrete future expiration milestones (~July 9, ~August 12) that prevent the 'arc complete / nothing concrete ahead' third-exit from firing; (3) gold has not broken down from near-record levels. However, at conviction 2 this basket is a residual structural stub, not a fresh trade — it must be outcompeted and culled by any stronger event on the shortlist. All four vehicles remain technically valid pure-plays on gold, but there is no basis for resizing or defending the position at this conviction level.
2025-05-23.exit_advice: Position is at the absolute cull floor (conviction 2) and will lose any fitness contest against fresher events. The only concrete milestones keeping this technically alive are the ~July 9 general-pause expiry and ~August 12 US-China truce expiry — monitor those dates. Exit immediately on any credible comprehensive deal announcement OR a sustained gold break below $3,000.

2025-05-30.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-05-30.exit_case: All three original catalyst legs (China/Canada/Mexico) de-escalated; EU delay confirms systematic risk-on tariff rollback.
2025-05-30.assessment: Week 16. The dominant headline is Trump's EU tariff delay (rttnews, 5/27), triggering US stocks to move 'sharply higher.' This is the fourth major de-escalation in six weeks — China leg cut 145%→30% (Week 14), Canada/Mexico on 90-day pause (Week 9), UK framework deal (Week 13), EU delayed now — and it is directionally the opposite of the original thesis. A risk-on equity surge on tariff relief is precisely the condition that drains gold's safe-haven premium. Devil's advocate case is the strongest of the 16-week run: all three named catalyst countries have been substantially de-escalated, and now even the EU (not in the original catalyst) is being walked back, confirming a systematic rollback pattern rather than isolated events. The hard exit bar is nonetheless not cleared: 30% China tariffs remain historically elevated, pass-through inflation persists (prior Walmart warnings), and the ~July 9 pause expiration is a concrete, datable forward event that prevents the 'arc complete / nothing concrete ahead' third-exit from firing. The Globe and Mail piece ('tariffs can sink Magnificent Seven for a decade') provides a residual structural-damage argument but contains zero explicit gold or miner linkage. Silence decay applies in full: no article this week mentions gold, GDX, AEM, GOLD, or NEM in the context of safe-haven demand. Conviction steps 2→1. All four vehicles remain technically valid pure-plays on gold prices, but the thesis has been soft-faded to the minimum survivable score across five consecutive weeks of structural de-escalation and silence. The position exists only as a July 9 option on re-escalation.
2025-05-30.exit_advice: At conviction 1 (absolute floor), this basket is a structural stub that must lose the fitness contest against any fresher event. Do not add to or defend the position; cull at the next rebalance if a higher-conviction catalyst is available. 2025-08-01.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-08-01.exit_case: China leg cut 145%→30% (May 12 truce), Canada/Mexico quietly rolled at July 9 — all three original named catalyst legs substantially de-escalated.
2025-08-01.assessment: Week 25. The 15-week silence breaks decisively on the escalation side. Two articles this week are unambiguously relevant: (1) businesstimes.com.sg (Aug 1) reports European stocks logged their 'biggest one-day drop in over three months' — i.e., since Liberation Day in April — after 'fresh US levies on dozens' of countries; (2) rttnews.com (Aug 1) confirms 'uncertainty over US reciprocal tariffs still lingers with less than a day remaining,' indicating a new tariff implementation deadline was expiring on or around August 1. Together these confirm a new escalation wave — Trump has imposed fresh levies on European and other countries, causing Liberation Day-scale equity market stress, entirely separately from the pending US-China truce expiry (~August 10–12). This is a genuine re-activation of the structural driver, not speculation: a specific, datable, new tariff action that produced measurable market disruption. The baystreet.ca piece (Jul 30) is a mixed signal: the TSX fell 170 points 'bogged down by gold and material stocks,' meaning miners underperformed on that specific day — a short-term headwind for GDX/AEM/GOLD/NEM, but not a thesis reversal given the broader tariff-stress context. The Bangkok Post (Jul 30) adds to the global tariff-escalation picture (Thai goods facing 19–20%). Silence decay reverses: prior conviction 1 steps to 4. Not a full reset because (a) no article explicitly links tariff escalation to gold safe-haven demand or any vehicle in the basket; (b) miners fell on the TSX that session; (c) the early/under-the-radar edge is long consumed — tariff-market stress is the most saturated macro narrative in global markets. The US-China 90-day truce expiry (~August 10–12) is now approximately 10 days away — the final concrete binary event in the entire arc. Week ending August 15 is the decisive inflection: confirmed China tariff snap-back or sustained re-escalation → conviction step-up and fresh thesis leg; permanent deal, confirmed extension, or continued silence through mid-August → hard exit. The 'arc complete / nothing concrete ahead' third-exit does not fire: the August 10–12 China binary is formally announced and imminent. All four vehicles (GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM) remain valid pure-plays on gold if this escalation wave sustains.
2025-08-01.exit_advice: Exit if US-China tariffs receive a permanent deal or confirmed extension at the ~August 10–12 expiry without re-escalation, OR if gold breaks down materially on confirmed de-escalation. Any week of continued silence after August 12 without a snap-back signal warrants hard exit.

2025-08-08.thesis: Trump's opening tariffs on China/Canada/Mexico ignite safe-haven gold surge
2025-08-08.exit_case: Markets edging up globally as sweeping tariffs take effect; risk-on absorption signals safe-haven gold premium draining rather than expanding.
2025-08-08.assessment: Week 26. The US-China 90-day truce expiry (~August 10-12) is now 2-4 days from these articles — the final formally-announced, datable binary event in the 26-week arc. This week's signal is structurally the most bearish for the thesis in several weeks. The dominant pattern: 'sweeping Trump tariffs take effect' (Marketscreener, Moneycontrol, Aug 7-8) and global markets are EDGING UP, not crashing. US markets opened higher Friday; Nasdaq surged 253 points mid-week; KOSPI surged past 3,200. This is the definition of tariff-shock absorption — the risk-off bid that drives gold's safe-haven premium is draining in real time, not expanding. The IANS piece (India -50% on select sectors, metal stocks leading losses) adds marginal stress, but miners falling in India is a headwind for GDX/AEM/GOLD/NEM, not a tailwind. The Miran Fed nomination creating dovish rate-cut expectations is indirectly gold-positive but not thesis-specific. Yahoo Finance (Energy Transfer) is entirely off-thesis. Critically, zero articles explicitly link tariff escalation to gold, safe-haven demand, or any vehicle in the basket. Silence decay applies: conviction 4→3. The Week 25 re-activation (European stocks' biggest drop since Liberation Day) is now being absorbed by a recovering market, weakening the structural-driver signal. The China August 10-12 binary is the sole surviving concrete anchor; the third-exit does not fire this week. Next week (ending August 15) is the decisive and final inflection: confirmed China tariff snap-back to ~145% + fresh gold/miner coverage → conviction step-up; permanent deal, confirmed extension, or continued silence through mid-August → hard exit, arc complete. All four vehicles (GDX, GOLD, AEM, NEM) remain technically valid pure-plays if re-escalation materialises.
2025-08-08.exit_advice: The US-China 90-day truce expiry (~August 10-12) is the sole remaining concrete anchor in the entire arc. If China tariffs do NOT snap back to ~145% — through a deal, extension, or silent rollover like the July 9 non-China deadline — the third-exit fires immediately: arc complete, nothing concrete ahead, crowd long departed. Exit all four vehicles on confirmed non-escalation through mid-August. If tariffs DO snap back to ~145% and fresh gold/miner/safe-haven coverage appears, step conviction up and reassess.

2025-10-17.thesis: China enacted rare-earth export curbs; Trump Nov. 1 100% tariff threat
2025-10-17.exit_case: China rare-earth export curbs already enacted before entry; initial supply-shock may be fully absorbed.
2025-10-17.assessment: First week. Two interlocking drivers: (1) China's rare-earth export curbs — enacted ~Oct 9-13, still very fresh at entry on Oct 17; they directly restrict the supply pipeline to US manufacturers and are NOT a temporary threat — they are a standing regime change that benefits domestic producers structurally. (2) Trump's Nov. 1 100% tariff threat — a concrete, datable, PENDING event that further closes the economics on Chinese supply and compresses the window for US buyers to lock in domestic sourcing. Japan Times confirms both legs explicitly. Coverage is currently in macro trade-tension framing (Asian equity sell-off), NOT in the specific 'buy MP/UUUU' mode that signals saturation — the pure-play rare-earth mining angle is still under the radar for US retail and generalist funds. MP Materials is the cleanest vehicle: sole large-scale US rare-earth miner (Mountain Pass), leveraged directly to both the curb and the tariff threat. UUUU (Energy Fuels) has a rare-earth processing line alongside uranium; weaker pure-play but a valid basket member since it explicitly processes US-sourced rare earths. No exit trigger has fired. Hold both into Nov. 1 and monitor for any US-China trade-deal language that rolls back the curbs.
2025-10-17.exit_advice: Exit if: (a) Nov. 1 tariff lands or is formally cancelled/deferred; (b) US-China trade truce explicitly reverses the rare-earth export curbs; (c) a broader deal reopens Chinese rare-earth supply to the West. Hold through silence — the Nov. 1 date is a live concrete scheduled event.

2026-01-23.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss-gold tariff + 125% China levy dislocate gold market
2026-01-23.exit_case: None found — tariffs are freshly imposed, NY-London $150 premium is active, no reversal or removal announced.
2026-01-23.assessment: First week of entry. The catalyst is live, specific, and named: a 39% US tariff on Swiss gold refiners and a 125% reciprocal levy on China have physically dislocated the gold market, driving NY futures to a ~$150 premium over London and pushing spot gold to a record ~$4,961. GDX is explicitly cited as surging. Coverage is concentrated in specialist outlets (carboncredits.com) rather than mainstream financial press, suggesting the thesis is not yet fully crowded. The tariffs remain in force with zero indication of reversal or negotiated carve-out. The dislocation is a structural supply-routing problem (physical bars cannot arbitrage freely when facing 39% import costs), so the premium and miner outperformance can persist as long as the tariffs stand. All five vehicles retain their thesis: GLD and GDX are the cleanest direct expressions; AEM, GOLD, and NEM benefit from elevated realized gold prices expanding margins. No reason to drop any vehicle this week. Modest conviction haircut because gold has already moved substantially (record highs), meaning some of the first-leg move is captured, but the ongoing arbitrage dislocation and tariff regime keep the trade alive.
2026-01-23.exit_advice: Exit when the 39% Swiss-gold tariff or 125% China levy is formally lifted/revoked, OR when the NY-London physical gold premium collapses back to near zero — either signals the dislocation has resolved.

2026-02-13.assessment: Seventeenth consecutive week of complete news silence on rare earths, China export curbs, MP Materials, and UUUU. The only tangentially relevant item is Reuters (2026-02-13) reporting Trump has signaled tariff de-escalation, unwinding safe-haven premiums in gold. This is the strongest exit argument in the entire 17-week arc: a broad US-China trade thaw reduces the economic pressure that drives domestic rare-earth sourcing urgency. However, it is a signal — not a formal rollback of China's enacted rare-earth export-curb regime, which was a distinct Chinese policy action independent of US tariff levels. Catalyst_resolved stays false; thesis_live remains technically true. The structural driver is real but has now encountered its first concrete headwind narrative. Conviction stays floored at 1 — silence-decay bottomed 11 weeks ago and the tariff de-escalation story adds no fresh upside milestone. Both vehicles (MP, UUUU) remain the correct basket if a fresh concrete milestone reactivates the driver (new curb escalation, US stockpile order, domestic sourcing contract), but at conviction 1 with 17 compounding silent weeks and a nascent de-escalation narrative, this position has zero competitive edge over any fresher event.
2026-02-13.exit_advice: Hold technically but position should be outcompeted by any fresher catalyst; the tariff de-escalation signal (Reuters 2026-02-13) is the first concrete headwind in 17 weeks — not a formal reversal of China's export-curb regime, but a meaningful erosion of the urgency leg. Do not add; let the mechanical optimizer cull.

2026-02-13.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss-gold tariff + 125% China levy dislocate gold market
2026-02-13.exit_case: Trump de-escalated tariff war; Reuters confirms NY physical gold premium is shrinking — core dislocation reverses.
2026-02-13.assessment: Three-week arc closes with a decisive, on-theme reversal signal. Entry on Jan 23 was built on the 39% Swiss gold tariff and 125% China levy creating a ~$150 NY-London futures premium and preventing free arbitrage of physical bars. Two consecutive silence weeks (Jan 30, Feb 6) decayed conviction from 6 to 4. This week Reuters delivers the exact reversal condition the exit rules demand: Trump has signaled de-escalation of the tariff conflict, the safe-haven bid is unwinding, and — critically — the New York physical gold premium is shrinking. The tariff orders may not yet be formally rescinded by executive order, but the market mechanism (premium) that was the whole thesis is demonstrably reversing in real time. This satisfies both the 'driving condition reversed' exit and the catalyst_resolved=true threshold. The three vehicles that benefited most from the premium and elevated spot (GLD, GDX, and the miners AEM, GOLD, NEM) all lose their thesis simultaneously. No vehicle retains a standalone case that survives this reversal. Full exit.
2026-02-13.exit_advice: Exit all positions immediately. Reuters (2026-02-13) explicitly states Trump signaled tariff de-escalation AND that the New York physical premium is actively shrinking. That premium was the entire mechanical heart of this trade. Close GLD, GDX, AEM, GOLD, NEM.

2026-03-20.thesis: US strikes Iran Kharg Island; Hormuz closure risk spikes tanker rates
2026-03-20.exit_case: Kharg Island strikes already occurred (~Mar 15-16); the initiating shock is backward-looking and may be priced into tanker stocks within days if no Hormuz disruption follows.
2026-03-20.assessment: Week 1 entry. US struck Iran's Kharg Island (its primary crude export terminal, handling ~90% of Iranian oil exports) around Mar 15-16. Confirmed by Sharecast (Mar 16): 'oil prices eased off highs, having risen again after the US bombing of Iran's vital oil hub, Kharg Island.' Oil is still advancing as of Mar 18, and markets are explicitly watching Hormuz (MarketScreener Mar 16: 'investors continued to watch developments in the Middle East, particularly the Strait of Hormuz'). The driving condition — Hormuz closure risk elevating tanker war-risk premiums and rerouting demand — is active and unresolved. No ceasefire, no strait closure confirmed or reopened, no Iranian retaliation or de-escalation announced. All four vehicles (FRO, INSW, NAT, TNK) are crude-tanker pure-plays that mechanically benefit from Middle East cargo-rerouting and rate spikes. Broad coverage is on oil/equities; the tanker-rate-spike angle is not yet mainstream-saturated. Early edge intact. Key monitor: Iranian military response, any Hormuz transit incident, or conversely a diplomatic back-channel. Conviction 7: acute, specific, high-magnitude, clean pure-play vehicles — but acute nature means failure-to-escalate could rapidly deflate the thesis.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: Exit on: confirmed US-Iran ceasefire or diplomatic de-escalation, explicit Hormuz reopening/normalisation statement, or tanker spot rates spike sharply then fully retrace with no further escalation catalyst visible. Hold through silence while Hormuz risk remains formally unresolved.

The Kharg Island strikes occurred (that was the entry event), but the LIVE driving condition was the escalation arc: continued US pressure → Iranian retaliation → Hormuz closure risk → tanker war-risk premiums. With Trump explicitly halting strikes, that arc is broken at the source. The ADNOC chief confirming 'expanded routes to avoid supply chain disruptions' further signals market adaptation rather than acute closure risk — the systemic shock is being absorbed and routed around, not amplified. Zero fresh tanker-rate or vehicle-specific coverage this week adds silence decay on top of the reversal signal. Week 1 journal explicitly noted: 'acute nature means failure-to-escalate could rapidly deflate the thesis.' That failure has now materialized as a deliberate policy halt. The thesis is over; hold-inertia would be the only reason to stay in.
2026-03-27.exit_advice: EXIT all positions immediately. The single driving condition — US escalation toward Iran creating Hormuz closure risk and tanker rate spikes — has been reversed by Trump halting further strikes. The acute catalyst has deflated exactly as Week 1 flagged as the primary failure mode.
